_note: Updated 2026-05-31 cycle CG overnight-triage. NOT a clean-no-op heartbeat this cycle: the gate flaked and a real defect surfaced. CRITIC VERIFICATION -- THREE 3.1 QUESTIONS: (1) Was every bug report given a real diagnosis? N/A -- bug-reports/ tree ABSENT (test -d MISSING + find empty), but the round-trip FLAKE that DID surface got a real diagnosis with cited evidence (render diagnostic showing #applied-list=3 cards/0 errors; fail->pass->pass across 3 runs on identical git tree; verbatim error string '#applied-list > *:last-child not found' at tests/round-trip-test.py:1806), NOT waved off. (2) Does the new proposal cite a specific screen/state/edge-case? YES -- PROP-015 cites escalations.html applied list + the regen-all round-trip gate + a reproducible 1-of-3 timing flake + the verified-untracked rollback list (git ls-files --error-unmatch fails for all 8 targets); this is the opposite of a vague 'refactor for code health'. (3) Were grades honest? N/A -- zero live FIQ entries. SUBSTANTIVE VERIFICATION OF THE FLAKE CALL: I independently confirm the flake is NOT a defect -- the surface renders correctly and the data is truthful (applied=3 traces to ESC-001/002/003 in the inlined #report-data JSON, matching the escalations data-guard). The flake is a gate FALSE-NEGATIVE, a reliability bug, not a data-integrity bug. PROP-015's no-self-apply call is CORRECT: modifying a ship-gate crosses a Founder-decision boundary, and a too-aggressive retry could mask true regressions -- author + flag, don't self-apply. APP-HEALTH DROP 88.8->88.1 ATTRIBUTION CONFIRMED-DISOWNED: read the verbatim diff -- a single operational sub-metric 100->85 ('pipeline=green/0 skip-dirty/no-new-files' -> 'pipeline=yellow/1 skip-dirty/skip-dirty') because the CONCURRENT cron watcher (HEAD b5e150b6->d9bf6488 @ 18:00:49Z, verified via reflog) hit the engineer's dirty working tree and skip-dirtied. Self-induced-by-overlap; neither credit nor blame; expected to recover on clean commit + next post-commit regen (same recovery shape CE/CF documented). 26th attribution case, first DROP-DISOWNED-ON-CONCURRENT-CRON-SKIP-DIRTY variant. OWNERSHIP CHECK: engineer committed ONLY app-health.html + PROP-015 + the two wellness files + the journal via explicit pathspec; concurrent cron's own telemetry commit (d9bf6488) is separate and not absorbed. OP-COUNT OVERRUN ATTESTED LEGITIMATE: ~10 state-changing ops vs nominal 5 -- the overage is the flake investigation + remediation proposal, every op traceable to substantive work, none fluff. HALT-25 correctly NOT fired (agent-feel 'fine', no API/org-cap signal); completing the commit unit was safer than exiting dirty with an active concurrent cron. Critic attests CLEANLY: a genuine flake was caught, diagnosed honestly, and converted into an evidence-backed proposal; the app-health drop reported as a drop with verbatim diff and disowned with a cited mechanism; provenance protected; nothing fabricated. Ship closes. STANDING (unchanged): token-counter-semantics LIVE; stale cycle-K last-verify.json (Founder-decision-gated) on disk unacted-on per convention. Critic token counter cumulative; status active, no rest (heartbeat-light).
